title: NBA plans AI system for automatic out-of-bounds calls
optimized_headline: NBA Introduces AI Technology for Instant Out-of-Bounds Decisions
raw_excerpt:
NBA Commissioner Adam Silver said the league plans to introduce an automated system for certain officiating decisions, including out-of-bounds calls. The system would use AI and cameras placed around the court to determine possession. Silver compared the approach to Hawk-Eye, the tracking technology used for line calls in tennis. raw_body:
NBA Commissioner Adam Silver said the league plans to introduce an automated system for certain officiating decisions, including out-of-bounds calls. The system would use AI and cameras placed around the court to determine possession. Silver compared the approach to Hawk-Eye, the tracking technology used for line calls in tennis. Disputed call preceded Silver’s comments Silver’s […]
The post NBA plans AI system for automatic out-of-bounds calls appeared first on AI News.
speedrun: The NBA is set to implement an AI-based system for officiating, particularly for out-of-bounds calls. This technology will utilize cameras around the court to accurately determine possession, similar to the Hawk-Eye system in tennis. Commissioner Adam Silver announced this initiative following a controversial call that highlighted the need for improved accuracy in officiating. This move could enhance the fairness of the game and reduce disputes over calls.
why_it_matters: Players and coaches could see fewer disputes during games, leading to a smoother experience on the court.
This shift may signal a broader trend toward technology-driven officiating in sports, potentially influencing how other leagues approach game management.

lenses.eli12: The NBA is planning to use AI to help referees make better calls during games, especially for out-of-bounds plays. It's like how tennis uses Hawk-Eye to track the ball's position. This could make games fairer and help players focus on playing rather than arguing about calls. Fans might enjoy watching a more accurate game.
lenses.pm: For product managers and founders, the NBA's move towards AI officiating highlights a growing user need for accuracy and efficiency in sports. By automating certain decisions, the league could reduce the costs associated with disputes and improve the overall game experience. This could inspire similar innovations in other industries where precision is critical.
lenses.engineer: The NBA's proposed system will leverage AI and camera technology to automate out-of-bounds calls, aiming for precision similar to Hawk-Eye in tennis. This system could involve advanced computer vision algorithms to analyze real-time footage and make split-second decisions. While the exact models and benchmarks aren't detailed, ensuring accuracy in dynamic environments like basketball poses unique technical challenges.
